_notes.history: DKN-AGOP consistently last. XGBoost dominant at 0.887. Key question: does depth help at all when d=10? rank is bounded by d, so ranks above 10 are physically meaningless — the AGOP M is at most rank-10.
_notes.time_budget: large n is the bottleneck. sigest_frac=0.3 on DKN-AGOP. D=500 only — higher D adds RFF cost with no representational benefit at d=10. n_iter capped at 15.

DKN-AGOP._comment_rank: d=10 means the AGOP M is 10×10 at most — rank values above 10 are identical to rank=10. Grid capped accordingly.

DKN-Align._comment_dk: d_k reduced to 50 (matching the natural dimensionality of this dataset) and batch_size increased to 1024 to exploit the large n.
